title: From Sun to Home Electricity
question: How does sunlight become useful electricity at home?
explanation: This pathway shows how solar energy is converted into electricity, connected to the grid and used in buildings.
challenge: Explain why solar panels alone are not enough without grids, storage or smart energy management.

title: From Wind to City Power
question: How does moving air support cleaner electricity for cities?
explanation: This pathway shows how kinetic energy from wind can become electrical power that supports urban demand.
challenge: Compare wind energy with solar energy. What makes each one stronger or weaker in different places?

title: From Coal to Climate Change
question: How does coal use contribute to climate change?
explanation: This pathway helps students trace the cause-and-effect chain from fossil fuel use to environmental impact.
challenge: Explain why this is not only a science issue, but also a social and policy issue.

title: Why Batteries Matter
question: Why does battery storage matter in renewable energy systems?
explanation: This pathway shows why storage helps match renewable supply with real energy demand.
challenge: Describe one situation where renewable energy would be wasted without storage.

title: From Sunlight to Food Chains
question: How does solar energy enter ecosystems?
explanation: This pathway links energy systems in nature to sustainability and ecology.
challenge: Explain why photosynthesis is one of the most important energy processes on Earth.

title: Electric Vehicles in the Wider System
question: Why do electric vehicles depend on the wider energy system?
explanation: This pathway shows that electric vehicles are part of a larger energy transition, not a stand-alone solution.
challenge: Why do electric vehicles only become truly low-carbon when the electricity system becomes cleaner too?

title: A Sustainable School Energy Plan
question: How can a school become more energy sustainable?
explanation: This pathway turns energy learning into a practical design challenge for students.
challenge: Choose one building improvement and explain how it reduces both cost and environmental impact.

title: Digital Life and Energy Use
question: How does digital life depend on energy systems?
explanation: This pathway helps students realise that invisible digital systems still depend on physical energy infrastructure.
challenge: Why do many people underestimate the energy behind everyday digital activities?
